text: Many workplaces now use artificial intelligence to handle repetitive tasks such as data entry or scheduling. This automation frees up employees to focus on more creative or strategic work. At the same time, productivity tools powered by AI help workers analyse information faster and make better decisions. However, AI is not replacing humans entirely; instead, we are seeing a shift toward human-AI collaboration. For example, a marketer might use AI to generate draft reports, then review and personalise the content. These changes mean some jobs will evolve, requiring new digital skills, but they also create opportunities for more meaningful and efficient work.
